---
title: "Batch & Document Management"
---

## Appending A Document To The Previous Document

You can append a document to the previous document. When you append the current document to the previous document, all the pages of the current document are added immediately after the last page of the previous document.

To append a document to the previous document:

1.  Click anywhere in the document area of the current document that you want to merge. The document is highlighted.
2.  In the upper-right corner of the document area, click the vertical ellipsis icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down menu is displayed.

3.  Click Append document to previous or press Alt + A. The document is appended to the previous document.

## Appending Selected Pages To The Previous Document

You can append selected pages of a document to the previous document. When you append the selected pages to the previous document, the pages of the current document are added immediately after the last page of the previous document.

To append selected pages to the previous document:

1.  On the relevant document area, do one of the following:
-
To select a single page, click the thumbnail image of the page that you want to merge and select the check box in the upper-left corner.

-
To select multiple pages, click the thumbnail image and select the check box in the upper-left corner of each thumbnail image. A tick appears on the selected pages.

-
To select all pages in a document, click the vertical ellipsis icon in the upper-right corner of the document area and then select Select all pages from the drop-down menu.

2.  In the upper-right corner of the document area, click the vertical ellipsis icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down menu is displayed.

3.  Click Append selected pages above or press Alt + P. The selected pages are appended to the previous document.

## Assigning A Document Class With The Snippet Viewer

The Snippet Viewer is a rectangular floating component which is displayed above the Document Viewer. The Snippet Viewer enables you to select the document class for a specific document after viewing the list of available document classes for the current batch. You can classify a document even if the Document List and Metadata Panel are collapsed.

For more information on document status and assignment of a document class based on the document status, see Document Status Types.

To assign a document class with the Snippet Viewer:

1.  Use the Tab key to navigate to the Snippet Viewer or click the Snippet Viewer area.
2.  In the Snippet Viewer, select the document class name or press F7. The available document classes are displayed.
3.  Type or select the relevant document class from the document class drop-down or use the keyboard shortcuts specified next to each document class. The document class is saved.  Note:
To cancel any erroneous text, click the Revert icon beside the document class or press Esc.

## Batch Concurrency

When a user opens a batch for the first time, the Owner field displays the name of the user. Unless the batch owner submits the batch or clicks Return to Batches, other users will not be able to open the batch and will receive the following error message: Batch [batch name] is locked by [name of owner]. However, batches that are locked by other users are not displayed when the Show batches to be verified check box is selected.

When an owner of a batch opens a batch and logs out of the application, without submitting it, the batch is unlocked and is available to other users for editing. However, if a batch owner exits the application abruptly by closing it, or it closes due to server outage, power outage or some other abnormal conditions, the batch remains in a locked state and is not available to other users for editing.

## Canceling A Batch Capture Process

You can cancel an in-progress batch capture process before all documents in the batch are captured. After canceling the process, you can either discard the portion of the batch that was already captured or prepare and submit this portion of the batch for verification or export.

Note:
If you cancel a sweep process that has been configured to move the local copies of its source files to a subfolder, only the local copies of the already-captured pages are moved to the subfolder when you submit the batch.

To cancel a batch capture process:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  Capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
4.  While documents in the batch are still being captured, click Cancel at the bottom of the layout. A confirmation message is displayed.
5.  Click Cancel Capture to confirm. The batch capture process is stopped, and the already-captured documents are displayed in the document review layout.
For information on discarding the already-captured portion of the batch, see Discarding a Batch.

For information on preparing the already-captured portion of the batch for verification or export, see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export.

## Capturing Documents

You can capture image or PDF documents into your Hyland Capture system by scanning or sweeping them as a batch. You can use either the panes or drop-down lists in the document capture layout to select the pre-configured capture setup options available to you. When you select a specific setup option in either its pane or drop-down list, this same option is automatically selected in the other location.

For more information on the specific setup options available in your environment (such as Content Queues or document classes), contact your system administrator.

Before capturing documents, also note the following:

-
It is considered a best practice to capture image documents at a resolution of 300 dpi or lower to keep file sizes manageable. Each captured page has a 10 MB maximum size limit. Where possible, oversized files are converted to JPEGs to reduce their size and allow their images to be displayed.

-
You can sweep single-page image, multi-page TIFF, and PDF documents. However, all other file types, as well as PDF XFA documents and password-protected PDFs or TIFFs, are not supported.

-
Individual documents, including PDFs and multi-page TIFFs, cannot exceed 999 pages.

-
When swept, PDFs and multi-page TIFFs are converted into single-page PNGs for further processing in the Hyland Capture application.

To capture documents:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  Select a Content Queue to group the batch documents to be captured under a common business function (for example, an HR Documents Content Queue to group W2 forms, cover letters, and performance reviews).
To select a Content Queue, do one of the following:

-
In the Content Queue pane, click the appropriate Content Queue in the list of available queues.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Tip:
To filter the list of available Content Queues, enter in the search field a string of characters included in the name of the Content Queue you want to select.

-
From the Content Queue drop-down list, select the appropriate Content Queue.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  To automatically apply the same document class to each document you scan or sweep into the batch, select a document class. This establishes a default classification for the batch, but you can change the classification of individual documents when you review them later in the capture process.
When working with document classes, also note the following:

-
If only one document class has been assigned to the selected Content Queue, that document class is selected by default. If multiple document classes have been assigned to the selected Content Queue, no document class is selected by default.

-
After selecting a document class, you can revert back to no selection by selecting Unclassified.

To select a document class, do one of the following:

-
In the Default Classification pane, select the appropriate document class from the drop-down list.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
From the classification drop-down list at the bottom of the document capture layout, select the appropriate document class.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Note:
The document class reverts to the default selection each time you change your Content Queue selection.

5.  Select a scanning device or sweep directory from which to import documents by doing one of the following:
-
In the Input Source pane, click the appropriate input source in the list of available input sources.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Tip:
To filter the list of available input sources, enter in the search field a string of characters included in the name of the input source you want to select.

-
From the Input Source drop-down list, select the appropriate input source.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Note:
You can manage the input sources available for selection by adding, editing, or deleting them. See Working with Input Sources for more information.

6.  Click Start Capture. The document review layout is displayed as the documents are captured into the system.
While capturing documents, also note the following:

-
Documents cannot be captured into the system if there is an issue with the selected input source (for example, the selected sweep directory is empty or the selected scanner is disconnected). If an error message is displayed, you can either cancel the process or resolve the issue (for example, by adding image or PDF files to the sweep directory or reconnecting the scanner) and click Resume or Retry to resume or retry the process.

-
If a document cannot be uploaded during a batch capture process (due to a network interruption, for example), an error message is displayed. You can either click Discard Batch to discard any previously uploaded documents and cancel the process, or you can click Retry Action to retry the process.

-
If a page cannot be uploaded during a batch capture process (due to firewall restrictions, network issues, or file size limits, for example), an error message is displayed and the erroring file is copied to an error folder in your sweep directory.

To resolve the issue, do any of the following:

- Click Copy error folder path to copy the error folder's location to the clipboard for simplified navigation.
- Click Details to expand or collapse details describing the page upload failure. With the details expanded, you can either click the Copy error details icon to copy the details to the clipboard or click the Save error details icon to save the details to a text file.
- To discard any previously uploaded pages and cancel the process, click Discard Batch.
- To skip the current page and continue the process, click Continue Capture. Any pages in a failed state are displayed in the document review layout with a warning icon overlay. These failed pages are not saved when the batch is submitted.
-
If you attempt to sweep any unsupported or oversized files into the system, these files are moved to an error folder in your sweep directory while the supported files are successfully captured. Once the process is complete, an error message is displayed.

To resolve the issue, do any of the following:

- Click Copy error folder path to copy the error folder's location to the clipboard for simplified navigation.
- Click Details to expand or collapse details describing which files could not be processed and why. With the details expanded, you can either click the Copy error details icon to copy the details to the clipboard or click the Save error details icon to save the details to a text file.
- To discard any previously uploaded pages or documents and cancel the process, click Discard Batch.
- If any files in the batch have been successfully swept into the system, you can click Continue Capture to return to the document review layout and review the batch.
For information on preparing the documents for verification or export, see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export.

## Deleting A Batch

You can delete batches only if you have access to the Administration interface. You can delete a maximum of 100 batches at a time.

To delete a batch:

1.  In the My Queues page select the check box corresponding to the batch you want to delete.  Note: To select all the batches, select the check box at the top of the list. Click Clear All to remove the selection.
2.  Click Delete.
3.  In the Delete Batch dialog box, click Continue. The batch is deleted successfully.  Note: You cannot delete a locked batch. If a batch fails to be deleted, a notification message appears in the notification center, containing the batch ID to assist in troubleshooting.

## Deleting A Document

You can delete a document from a batch if that document is erroneously added to the batch.

Note:
If you delete all documents in the batch, the batch is deleted automatically.

To delete a document:

1.  Click anywhere in the document area of the relevant document that you want to delete. The document is highlighted.
2.  In the upper-right corner of the document area, click the vertical ellipsis icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down menu is displayed.

3.  Click Delete document or press Alt + Delete. The document is deleted from the batch.

## Deleting Pages From A Document

You can delete selected pages from a document.

To delete selected pages:

1.  On the relevant document area, do one of the following:
-
To select a single page, click the thumbnail image of the page you want to delete and select the check box in the upper-left corner.

-
To select multiple pages, click the thumbnail image and select the check box in the upper-left corner of each thumbnail image. A tick appears on the selected pages.

2.  In the upper-right corner of the document area, click the vertical ellipsis icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down menu is displayed.

3.  Click Delete selected pages or press Delete. The relevant pages are deleted from the document.

## Discarding A Batch

You can discard a batch if you decide not to submit it for verification or export. This simultaneously deletes all documents in the batch and the batch itself.

To discard a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  Click Discard Batch at the bottom of the layout. A confirmation message is displayed.
4.  Click Discard Batch to confirm. The document capture layout is displayed. For information on capturing a new batch, see Capturing Documents.

## Document Status Types

When a batch is transferred to the Hyland Experience Capture Verify Data interface for further processing, each document inside the batch has a current status which may be different from that of the other documents in the batch.

The status of a document depends on various parameters, such as:

-
whether a default or custom document class is assigned to the document

-
whether the class assigned requires modification or confirmation, and

-
whether the document metadata fields require manual verification or not.

The name of the default document class may vary based on the Content Queue to which the document is mapped.

The following table describes the different types of document classes that are available. The class name "AP Invoice" is used as an example to indicate the high confidence status types.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Default Class Assigned | Need to change or confirm document class? | Need to verify metadata fields |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Yes | Yes |
| The assigned class appears in black bold font with a red eye beside it. | No | Yes |
| The assigned class with verified fields appears in black bold font with a green tick beside it. | No | No |

**Document Name**
Hyland Capture
**Department/Group**
Documentation
**Revision Number**
14.0

## Export

The following endpoints can be used for exporting batches, documents, and their metadata out of the Hyland Capture application:

-  Return a List of Batches for Export
-  Return Batch Metadata
-  Return Document Metadata
-  Mark a Batch for Deletion

## Exporting A Batch

Once you have acquired an access token (see Getting Started), you can start exporting batches and their metadata out of the Hyland Capture application.

After a user has finished processing a batch, you can download a ZIP file containing the batch's metadata and PDF files of all documents in the batch. Optionally, you can download individual documents instead of the entire batch. Once you have finished downloading the batch documents and metadata, mark the exported batch as complete so it can be deleted in the application by background processes.

Note: This tutorial makes calls to Export API endpoints. For full reference information on all available Export API endpoints, see Export.
To export a batch:

1.  Open Command Prompt or Terminal.
2.  Retrieve a list of batches ready for download by entering a request similar to the following example:
```
curl -i -X GET [base_url]/api/export/batch/list?first=5 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer [access_token]" \
  -H "User-Agent: [product]/[version]" \
  -H "hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a" \
  -H "hxp_app: hxc" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"
```

where the placeholders represent the following:

| Placeholder | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [base_url] | The URL of your Hyland Capture environment |
| [access_token] | The access token you retrieved when you completed authentication |
| [product] | The name of the software you are using to make the request |
| [version] | The numbered version of the software |

A response that includes the list of retrieved batch IDs is displayed, as shown in the following example:

```
{
  "Batches": [
    {
      "ID": "635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529"
    },
    {
      "ID": "635e1123c43252b2sdjn3530"
    },
    {
      "ID": "635e1123c43252b2sdjn3533"
    },
    {
      "ID": "635e1123c43252b2sdjn3536"
    },
    {
      "ID": "635e1123c43252b2sdjn3537"
    }
  ],
  "TotalBatches": 5
}
```

3.  Retrieve a download URL for the batch you want to export by entering a request similar to the following example, using the appropriate batch ID from the previous response:
```
curl -i -X GET [base_url]/api/export/batch?batchId=635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer [access_token]" \
  -H "User-Agent: [product]/[version]" \
  -H "hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a" \
  -H "hxp_app: hxc" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"
```

A response that includes the download URL for the batch and IDs for the documents in the batch is displayed, as shown in the following example:

```
{
  "BatchId": "string",
  "BatchName": "string",
  "ContentQueueId": "string",
  "ContentQueueName": "string",
  "DownloadUrl": "string",
  "Checksum": "string",
  "ChecksumAlgorithm": "string",
  "DocumentIds": [
    "663225c5cecd4546ecdc21f4",
    "663225ce5d008d0cd044d200",
    "663225d2af7e54ec1f32032a"
  ],
  "DeletedFiles": [
    {
      "FileId": "string",
      "ContentType": "string",
      "OriginalFileName": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

4.  To download individual documents instead of the entire batch, enter a request similar to the following example, using the appropriate document IDs from the previous response:
```
curl -i -X GET [base_url]/api/export/batch/documents?batchId=635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529&docId=663225c5cecd4546ecdc21f4 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer [access_token]" \
  -H "User-Agent: [product]/[version]" \
  -H "hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a" \
  -H "hxp_app: hxc" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"
```

A response that includes the download URL for each specified document is displayed, as shown in the following example:

```
{
  "Documents": [
    {
      "Id": "663225c5cecd4546ecdc21f4",
      "Name": "string",
      "BatchName": "string",
      "ContentQueueName": "string",
      "DocumentClass": {
        "Id": "string",
        "Name": "string"
      },
      "FieldValues": [
        {
          "Id": "string",
          "Name": "string",
          "DisplayValue": "string",
          "SourceValue": "string",
          "DataType": "Alphanumeric"
        }
      ],
      "ExportedFiles": [
        {
          "FileName": "string",
          "ContentType": "string",
          "DownloadUrl": "string",
          "Checksum": "string",
          "ChecksumAlgorithm": "string",
          "OriginalFiles": [
            {
              "Id": "string",
              "FileName": "string",
              "ContentType": "string",
              "Rotation": "string",
              "Skew": 0
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

5.  To download a ZIP file containing the batch's metadata and PDF files of documents in the batch, navigate to the download URL returned in the previous response for the batch or individual documents.
6.  Mark the exported batch as complete, or ready for deletion, by entering a request similar to the following example:
```
curl -i -X PATCH [base_url]/api/export/complete?batchId=635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer [access_token]" \
  -H "User-Agent: [product]/[version]" \
  -H "hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a" \
  -H "hxp_app: hxc"
```

For more information on the request parameters and response body elements shown in these examples, see Export.

## Indexing A Batch

If you captured documents in a Content Queue that has been configured to bypass the Hyland Capture Verify Data interface, you can index the batch using the Metadata Panel on the right side of the document review layout. This prepares the batch for direct export out of the Hyland Capture application.

When preparing to index a batch, also note the following:

-
If no fields have been assigned to any document classes in the batch's assigned Content Queue, the batch does not require indexing and the Metadata Panel is not displayed. For information on submitting the batch for export, see Submitting a Batch.

-
If any fields have been configured for bar code extraction and assigned to document classes in the batch's assigned Content Queue, metadata values are automatically extracted from bar codes on the captured documents and assigned to the corresponding fields during batch processing. Unclassified documents do not undergo bar code extraction processing.

To index a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  In the thumbnail viewer to the right of the document viewer, set the focus to the document you want to index (see Organizing Captured Documents).
4.  To expand the Metadata Panel on the right side of the layout, click the Show Document Fields icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
To collapse the Metadata Panel along the right side of the layout, click the Hide Document Fields icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  To classify or reclassify the document, do one of the following:
-
In the thumbnail viewer, select a document class from the classification drop-down list in the upper-left corner of the document bar. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking its arrow or pressing F7.

-
In the expanded Metadata Panel, select a document class from the Classification drop-down list near the top. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking its arrow.

When reclassifying a document, also note the following:

-
Each time you change the document's classification, previously entered metadata values are retained for common fields or cleared for unique fields. Fields that have the same name but different definitions or IDs are considered unique.

- Manually changing a document class does not trigger any additional bar code extraction processing, if applicable, for the document.
Once you select a document class for the document, the metadata fields associated with that document class are displayed in the Metadata Panel, as shown in the following example:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The document's local ID is displayed at the top of the panel.

6.  Enter valid metadata values for the document in the corresponding fields of the Metadata Panel. If an invalid value is entered, or if no value is entered for a required field, an error message is displayed in red text below the field. For more information on metadata handling and validation, see Metadata Handling and Validation.
7.  To save the entered value for a metadata field, defocus the field by doing one of the following:
-
Click outside the field.

-
Press Tab to shift the focus to the next field on the document.

-
Press Enter to shift the focus to the next field requiring review in the document, or to the next field requiring review in the batch if no remaining fields in the document require review. A field requires review if it contains an invalid value or if it is a required field that contains no value.

When working with saved metadata values, also note the following:

-
If you split pages from the current document into a new document, any assigned document class or metadata values from the original document are also applied to the new document.

-
If you merge pages from the current document into another document in the batch, the merged pages inherit any existing document class or metadata values from the destination document.

8.  Index additional documents in the batch as needed. Once all entered metadata is valid and all required metadata fields on all documents in the batch have been indexed, you can submit the batch for export (see Submitting a Batch).

## Mark A Batch For Deletion

Marks a batch as complete after it has been exported. This queues the completed batch to be deleted from the application by background processes.

## Base Request

```
PATCH /api/export/complete
```

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Description | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| batchId | Alphanumeric ID for the batch. | string | Required | None |

## Headers

See Export for headers details.

## Sample Request

```
PATCH /api/export/complete?batchId=635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529

Authorization: Bearer [access_token]
User-Agent: MyCompanyProduct/1.0
hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a
hxp_app: hxc
```

## Response

No response body is returned.

## Status Codes and Errors

The following table lists the returned status codes.

| Code | Description | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 204 | No Content | The batch is marked for deletion. |
| 404 | Not Found | The specified batch could not be found. |
| 409 | Conflict | The specified batch is not in a state that is ready to be exported. |

## Opening The Next Invalid Batch

The Continue to next batch check box enables you to open the next invalid batch directly after submitting the current batch. This batch belongs to the same Content Queue as the batch that is currently being verified. The check box is visible only when either the Document List or Metadata Panel is open.

To open the next invalid batch:

1.  Select the Continue to next batch check box at the bottom of the Document List.
2.  Click Submit.  Note:
If there are no invalid batches available in the Content Queue, the batch list page is displayed.

## Organizing Captured Documents

You can organize captured documents in the thumbnail viewer of the document review layout. Organizing may include classifying the documents, rearranging the pages within those documents, or deleting pages or documents.

To organize captured documents in a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  In the thumbnail viewer to the right of the document viewer, set the focus to the page or document you want to organize by doing one of the following:
-
Click the page or document.

-
Use the arrow keys.

Both the focused page and the focused document are outlined in blue. In the following example, the focus is set to the first page of the first document:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Each document is displayed in the thumbnail viewer as a row, or document bar. The batch name is displayed in the upper-left corner of the viewer, and the numbers of documents and total pages in the batch are displayed in the upper-right corner of the viewer. The classification of each document is displayed in the upper-left corner of its document bar, and the number of pages in the document is displayed in the upper-right corner of its document bar.

Note: When pages in a document are still being processed, a progress spinner icon and text describing the action in progress (for example, Scanning Pages or Auto Classification) are displayed temporarily in the upper-left corner of the document bar. The actions are color coded: purple for document capture, orange for classification, and green for extraction. You cannot make changes to a document while it is undergoing bar code extraction (Auto Extraction).
When setting the focus, also note that unless you change the focus manually, it is set by default in the following ways:

-
If documents in the batch are still being captured, the focus is automatically set to the most recently captured page. If you changed the focus manually, you can reset the automatic focus back to the most recently captured page by clicking Return to Live in the upper-right corner of the document viewer.

-
Once a batch capture process has been completed or canceled, the focus is automatically set to the first page of the first document.

-
If you are scanning additional documents into the batch (see Scanning Additional Documents), the focus is automatically set back to the most recently captured page (without having to click Return to Live). Once the additional scanning is complete, the focus is set to the first page of the first additional scanned document.

-
If the Metadata Panel is displayed for indexing (see Indexing a Batch), the focus is set to the first blank, required metadata field on the focused document. If there are no blank, required metadata fields on the focused document, the focus is set to the first metadata field; if this first field already contains a value, the value is selected when the focus is set.

4.  To classify, reclassify, or unclassify the document, select a classification option from the classification drop-down list in the upper-left corner of the document bar. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking its arrow or pressing F7.
When reviewing document classification options, also note the following:

-
If the document class has been automatically assigned according to a bar code on the document, a bar code icon is displayed next to the classification drop-down list.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
If you make any changes to this document class assignment, the bar code icon is removed from the document bar.

-
If bar code extraction has been performed in conjunction with bar code classification, a red or green icon is displayed to the left of the classification drop-down list to indicate confidence levels in the assigned document class and metadata values (see Document Status Types for more information).

-
Shortcut keys are listed to the right of their assigned classifications in the classification drop-down list. Once you know the shortcut keys for the available classifications, you can use these shortcut keys to more quickly classify documents.

5.  Select the pages you want to organize by doing one of the following:
-
To select a specific page, set the focus to the page and then do one of the following:

-
Click the blue square in the upper-left corner of the page.

-
Press Enter.

-
To select multiple pages in the document, do one of the following:

-
For non-consecutive pages, hold Ctrl and click each page.

-
For consecutive pages, hold Shift and click the first and last pages in the series.

If the focus is already set to the first or last page you want to select, you can hold Shift and just click the page at the other end of the series.

-
To select all pages in the document simultaneously, do one of the following:

-
Select Select all pages from the separation options drop-down list. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking the vertical ellipsis icon in the upper-right corner of the document bar or pressing F9.

-
Press Alt + C.

A check mark is displayed in the upper-left corner of each selected page in the document, as shown in the following example.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Note:
Pages can only be selected in the currently focused document. A selected page becomes deselected if you select the page again, select a separation option, or change the focus to a different document.

6.  To move the selected page or pages to a different position, drag the pages to the current position of another page in the batch. The dragged pages are moved to this position, and the other pages in the document are shifted left or right to make room for the dragged pages.
If you drag more than one page at a time, note the following:

-
The number of pages you are dragging is displayed over the thumbnail as you drag it.

-
The dragged pages retain their order when you drop them in the new position.

7.  To append the selected pages to the previous document in the batch, do one of the following:
-
To append a specific page or pages, select Append selected pages to above from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + P.

-
To append an entire document, select Append document to previous from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + A.

8.  To split pages from the current document into a new document, do one of the following:
-
To split all pages from the focused page to the end of the document, select Split from current page from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + S.

-
To split only the selected pages, select Split selected pages from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + L.

9.  To delete the selected pages from the batch, do one of the following:
-
To delete a specific page or pages, select Delete selected pages from the separation options drop-down list, or press Delete.

Note: If the document contains any bar code or blank pages that have been marked for deletion, each of these pages is outlined in red, and an indicator icon is displayed next to the document's total number of pages in the upper-right corner of the document bar. These marked pages are automatically deleted when you submit the batch.
-
To delete an entire document, select Delete document from the separation options drop-down list, or press Alt + Delete.

Note:
If you delete every document in the batch, the batch is also deleted.

For information on indexing the organized batch in the document review layout, see Indexing a Batch.

For information on submitting the organized batch for verification or export, see Submitting a Batch.

## Preparing A Batch For Verification Or Export

Once you have captured documents into your Hyland Capture system, you can review and organize these documents in the document review layout. You can zoom, scale, and rotate each page of the captured documents in the document viewer on the left side of the layout. You can also append, split, delete, and classify the captured documents in the thumbnail viewer to the right of the document viewer.

If necessary, you can scan additional documents into a pending, scanned batch without having to return to the document capture layout.

If you captured documents in a Content Queue that has been configured to bypass the Hyland Capture Verify Data interface, you can also index the batch using the Metadata Panel on the right side of the document review layout. This prepares the batch for direct export out of the application. Otherwise, the batch can be indexed later once it is sent to the Verify Data interface. In either case, you can submit the batch when you are ready to process it out of the document review layout.

When preparing a batch for verification or export, also note the following:

- Once the document review layout is displayed after you start capturing batch documents, you cannot return to the document capture layout until you submit or delete the pending batch.
- You must maintain a successful connection to the Capture Desktop service, or the service on which the Desktop Host runs, to perform actions on documents and pages in the captured batch. If you lose your connection while performing an action, an error message is displayed.

See the following sections for details on preparing a batch for verification or export:

-
Reviewing Captured Pages

-
Organizing Captured Documents

-
Scanning Additional Documents

-
Indexing a Batch

-
Submitting a Batch

-
Discarding a Batch

-
Canceling a Batch Capture Process

## Reordering Pages

You can reorder pages in and across documents using the thumbnail images. You can drag a thumbnail image from its current position and drop it to its new position. You can drag and drop multiple pages. When you drag and drop a page, a blue visual indicator appears to assist you with the new drop position.

To reorder pages:

1.  On the relevant document area, do one of the following:
-
To select a single page, click the thumbnail image of the page that you want to move.

-
To select multiple pages, click the thumbnail image and select the check box in the upper-left corner of each thumbnail image. A tick appears on the selected pages.

You can also select multiple pages in a range by clicking on a page, pressing and holding Shift, and clicking on the page that marks the end of the range. You can then press Ctrl and click the relevant page to cancel the selection.

2.  Drag and drop the thumbnail images to their new position by placing the page within the visual drop indicator boundary. The corresponding document is updated and the new sequence is saved.  Note:
You can also reorder pages by using common keyboard shortcuts, such as Ctrl + X for cut and Ctrl + V for paste.

## Return A List Of Batches For Export

Returns a list of available batches that are ready to exported from the application.

## Base Request

```
GET /api/export/batch/list
```

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Description | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| contentQueueId | Alphanumeric ID for the Content Queue by which to filter the available batches. | string | Optional | None |
| first | The number of available batches by which to limit the search results. | integer | Optional | Valid values range from 1 to 1000. Default is 10. |

## Headers

See Export for headers details.

## Sample Request

```
GET /api/export/batch/list?first=5

Authorization: Bearer [access_token]
User-Agent: MyCompanyProduct/1.0
hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a
hxp_app: hxc
Accept: application/json
```

## Response Body

| Element | Description | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Batches | List of the batches ready for export. | array | None |
| ID | Alphanumeric ID of the batch. | string | None |
| TotalBatches | The total number of batches ready to be exported. | integer | None |

## Sample Response

```
{
  "Batches": [
    {
      "ID": "string"
    }
  ],
  "TotalBatches": 0
}
```

## Status Codes and Errors

The following table lists the returned status codes.

| Code | Description | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | Returns the list of batches that are ready to be exported. |
| 404 | Not Found | The Content Queue with the specified ID could not be found. |

## Return Batch Metadata

Returns metadata for the specified batch. These metadata include a URL pointing to a ZIP file that contains the batch's data.

## Base Request

```
GET /api/export/batch
```

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Description | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| batchId | Alphanumeric ID for the batch. | string | Required | None |

## Headers

See Export for headers details.

## Sample Request

```
GET /api/export/batch?batchId=635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529

Authorization: Bearer [access_token]
User-Agent: MyCompanyProduct/1.0
hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a
hxp_app: hxc
Accept: application/json
```

## Response Body

| Element | Description | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BatchId | Alphanumeric ID for the batch. | string | None |
| BatchName | The name of the batch. | string | None |
| ContentQueueId | Alphanumeric ID for the Content Queue under which the batch resides. | string | None |
| ContentQueueName | The name of the Content Queue under which the batch resides. | string | None |
| DownloadUrl | The signed URL of the exported ZIP file for the batch. | string | None |
| Checksum | The hashed value of the ZIP file. | string | None |
| ChecksumAlgorithm | Algorithm used to calculate the hash of the ZIP file. | string | Common values include SHA-256. |
| DocumentIds | List of alphanumeric IDs for the documents in the batch. | array | None |
| DeletedFiles | List of files that have been deleted from the batch. | array | None |
| FileId | Alphanumeric ID of the deleted file. | string | None |
| ContentType | The type of content the deleted file represents. | string | This value maps to the MIME type set by the client (for example, image/png). |
| OriginalFileName | The original file name of the deleted file. | string | None |

## Sample Response

```
{
  "BatchId": "string",
  "BatchName": "string",
  "ContentQueueId": "string",
  "ContentQueueName": "string",
  "DownloadUrl": "string",
  "Checksum": "string",
  "ChecksumAlgorithm": "string",
  "DocumentIds": [
    "string"
  ],
  "DeletedFiles": [
    {
      "FileId": "string",
      "ContentType": "string",
      "OriginalFileName": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Status Codes and Errors

The following table lists the returned status codes.

| Code | Description | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | Returns the metadata for the specified batch. |
| 404 | Not Found | The specified batch could not be found. Error details may include the following: ItemNotFound. |
| 409 | Conflict | The specified batch is not in a state that is ready to be exported. Error details may include the following: InvalidBatchState. |
| 500 | Internal Server Error | The batch artifact and ZIP file hashes do not match. Error details may include the following: ChecksumMismatch. |

## Return Document Metadata

Returns a list of metadata for the specified documents in the specified batch. These metadata include URLs pointing to PDF files for the documents.

## Base Request

```
GET /api/export/batch/documents
```

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Description | Type | Required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| batchId | Alphanumeric ID for the batch. | string | Required | None |
| docId | List of the alphanumeric IDs for the documents in the batch. | array | Required | The list must contain a minimum of one and a maximum of 30 document IDs. |

## Headers

See Export for headers details.

## Sample Request

```
GET /api/export/batch/documents?batchId=635e1123c43252b2sdjn3529&docId=[635e1123c43252b2sdjn3530,635e1123c43252b2sdjn3531]

Authorization: Bearer [access_token]
User-Agent: MyCompanyProduct/1.0
hxp_environment: test-ac7a0b36-4fca-3849-ga68-13e6h6385c8a
hxp_app: hxc
Accept: application/json
```

## Response Body

-
-
-
-
-

-
-
-
-

| Element | Description | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Documents | List of the specified documents in the batch. | array | None |
| Id | Alphanumeric ID of the parent element. | string | The ID may represent a document, document class, field value, or original file. |
| Name | The name of the parent element. | string | The name may represent a document, document class, or field value. |
| BatchName | The name of the specified batch. | string | None |
| ContentQueueName | The name of the Content Queue under which the batch resides. | string | None |
| DocumentClass | List of elements identifying the document class to which the document is assigned. | array | Commonly used elements in the list may include the document class ID and name. |
| FieldValues | List of values representing each document's fields. | array | Commonly used elements in the list may include the field ID and name. |
| DisplayValue | The field value that is displayed to the user after it has been formatted. | string | None |
| SourceValue | The text value of the field as it is provided by the source. | string | Common examples of sources providing the text values include OCR and bar code processing. |
| DataType | An enumeration of the data types used in the application. | string | Common data types include the following:   Alphanumeric   Phrase   Numeric   DateTime   CurrencyAmount |
| ExportedFiles | List of the files that have been exported from the application. | array | None |
| FileName | The name of the file. | string | Depending on the parent element, the file name may represent an exported file or an original batch file. |
| ContentType | The type of content the file represents. | string | This value maps to the MIME type set by the client (for example, image/png). Depending on the parent element, the content type may represent an exported file or an original batch file. |
| DownloadUrl | The pre-signed download URL of the file. | string | None |
| Checksum | The hashed value of the exported PDF file. | string | None |
| ChecksumAlgorithm | Algorithm used to calculate the hash of the PDF file. | string | Common values include SHA-256. |
| OriginalFiles | List of the original batch file details for the specified document. | array | None |
| Rotation | The rotation angle of the original document. | string | Possible values include the following:  None   Clockwise   UpsideDown   CounterClockwise |
| Skew | The skew angle of the original document. | floating point | Possible values range from -90.00 to 90.00 degrees. |

## Sample Response

```
{
  "Documents": [
    {
      "Id": "string",
      "Name": "string",
      "BatchName": "string",
      "ContentQueueName": "string",
      "DocumentClass": {
        "Id": "string",
        "Name": "string"
      },
      "FieldValues": [
        {
          "Id": "string",
          "Name": "string",
          "DisplayValue": "string",
          "SourceValue": "string",
          "DataType": "Alphanumeric"
        }
      ],
      "ExportedFiles": [
        {
          "FileName": "string",
          "ContentType": "string",
          "DownloadUrl": "string",
          "Checksum": "string",
          "ChecksumAlgorithm": "string",
          "OriginalFiles": [
            {
              "Id": "string",
              "FileName": "string",
              "ContentType": "string",
              "Rotation": "string",
              "Skew": 0
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Status Codes and Errors

The following table lists the returned status codes.

| Code | Description | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | Returns the list of metadata for the specified documents in the specified batch. |
| 404 | Not Found | The specified batch or documents could not be found. Error details may include the following: ItemNotFound. |
| 409 | Conflict | The specified batch is not in a state that is ready to be exported. Error details may include the following: InvalidBatchState. |
| 500 | Internal Server Error | The document artifact and PDF file hashes do not match. Error details may include the following: ChecksumMismatch. |

## Reviewing Captured Pages

You can review any page of the captured batch documents in the image viewer on the left side of the document review layout. Reviewing a page may include adjusting its display size or position to allow you to more effectively assess the page's image quality and content.

To review captured pages in a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.  Note:
If documents in the batch are still being captured, the focus is automatically set to the most recently captured page unless you change the focus manually.

3.  In the thumbnail viewer on the right side of the layout, set the focus to the page you want to review by clicking the page or using the arrow keys (see Organizing Captured Documents for more information). The page is displayed in the image viewer on the left side of the layout, as shown in the following example:   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  To change the way the page is scaled in the image viewer, select one of the following fit modes:

| Fit Mode | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Fit Content | Fit Content displays the entire page of a document in the viewer. |
| Fit Height | Fit Height displays the pages of the document at full height. |
| Fit Width | Fit Width displays the pages of the document at full width. |
| Actual Size | Actual Size displays the page at its actual size. |
| Manual | Manual is automatically selected when the zoom level of the page is changed. |

5.  To change the zoom level of the page, do one of the following:
-
To zoom in or out on the page, select the + or - icons. You can also press the + or - keys.

-
To scale the page to a certain percentage of the original document, enter a percent value in the field. In the following example, the page will be scaled to 18% of its original size:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
6.  To rotate the page, select one of the following options:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Rotation Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Rotate Left | Rotates the page 90 degrees counter-clockwise. You can also press Ctrl + Left. |
| Rotate 180 | Rotates the page 180 degrees. You can also press Ctrl + Down. |
| Rotate Right | Rotates the page 90 degrees clockwise. You can also press Ctrl + Right. |

7.  To display the page in a fullscreen view, select the Fullscreen icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
To exit fullscreen view, press Esc or select the Exit Fullscreen icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
8.  To pan across a page, click and hold the mouse pointer and then drag the mouse across the page.
9.  Review additional pages in the batch as needed. For information on organizing pages and documents in the batch, see Organizing Captured Documents.

## Scanning Additional Documents

When you have a pending batch that was captured using a scan input source in the document review layout, you can use any existing scan input source to scan additional documents into the batch under the same Content Queue.

Note: If any fields have been configured for bar code extraction and assigned to document classes in the batch's assigned Content Queue, bar code extraction is performed on any newly scanned documents, but it is not performed again on any previously captured documents in the batch.
To scan additional documents into a pending batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents using a scan input source to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  Above the thumbnail viewer on the right side of the layout, click the Scan More Pages icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The Capture Toolbar is displayed, showing the batch's assigned Content Queue on the left.

4.  To automatically apply the same document class to each new document you scan into the batch, select a document class from the classification drop-down list.
5.  Select a scan input source from the Input Source drop-down list.
6.  Click Start Capture. The new documents are captured into the system and added to the end of the batch.  Tip:
To ensure the newly captured documents are properly prepared for verification, organize or reorganize the batch documents once the new documents have been added (see Organizing Captured Documents).

## Splitting A Document From The Current Page

You can split a document to create a new document by selecting the thumbnail image of the page from which the document will be split. A new document is created with the selected page and all the pages succeeding the selected page.

To split the document from the selected page:

1.  Select the relevant page in the document by clicking anywhere on the thumbnail image of that page. The page is highlighted.
2.  In the upper-right corner of the document area, click the vertical ellipsis icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down menu is displayed.

3.  Click Split from current page or press Alt + S. A new document is created with the selected page and all the pages succeeding the selected page.

## Submitting A Batch

Once you have reviewed and organized your captured documents, you can submit the batch for verification or export. This clears the batch from the document review layout and sends it to either the Hyland Capture Verify Data interface or an export package.

To submit a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  Click Submit Batch in the lower-right corner of the layout. The document capture layout is displayed with one of the following notifications:
-
A green notification indicating the batch has been submitted successfully. This notification is automatically cleared after a few seconds.

-
A yellow notification indicating a subfolder cannot be created for a sweep process. This only applies to sweep processes configured to move the local copies of your source files after sweeping the files into the system. The notification may be displayed if you do not have rights to create subfolders in the sweep directory (contact your system administrator for assistance).

The batch is submitted successfully, but the local source files remain in the original sweep directory. The notification is automatically cleared after a few seconds.

-
A red notification indicating the batch submission has failed. This may be caused by a temporary issue with server processing or authentication. While this notification is displayed, the document capture layout is disabled.

4.  If the batch submission fails, click OK to close the red notification and enable the document capture layout. The batch is automatically resubmitted a few times over a five-minute period to give the temporary background issues a chance to clear. If the issues persist beyond this period, the batch is placed in a dormant status.  Note:
If you refresh the page or reopen the Hyland Capture application, any pending failure notification is displayed again. You must click OK to close the message and enable the document capture layout.

For information on verifying a successfully submitted batch, see Verify Data Usage Overview.

For information on processing an export package into OnBase, see the Hyland Experience Capture Import Processor documentation.

## The Document List Toolbar

The Document List toolbar, located at the top of the page, enables you to view the batch identification number, and navigate between documents.

The following table summarizes the elements inside the Document List toolbar:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Toolbar Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Doc ID | Displays the identification number of the selected document. |
|  | Displays the first document in the batch. |
|  | Displays the previous document in the batch. |
| <Current document>/<Total number of documents> | Type the number of the page where you want to navigate. |
|  | Displays the next document in the batch. |
|  | Displays the last document in the batch. |
|  | Collapses the Document List and displays it as a vertical bar on the left. |

## The Document Viewer Toolbar

The Document Viewer toolbar is located at the bottom of the Document Viewer. This toolbar enables you to adjust the display magnification of each document page, rotate a page, and also navigate between document pages using the following commands.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Toolbar Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Displays the first page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
|  | Displays the previous page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
| `<Current page>/<Total number of pages>` | Type the number of the page where you want to navigate. |
|  | Displays the next page in document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
|  | Displays the last page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
|  | Zooms out. You can also press Alt and -. |
|  | Zooms in. You can also press Alt and +. |
|  | Rotates the image to the left. You can also press Ctrl + Left. |
|  | Rotates the image to the right. You can also press Ctrl + Right. |
|  | Fits the current image to the width or height of the Document Viewer area so that maximum enlargement is possible. You can also press Alt + B. |
|  | Fits the current image to the height of the Document Viewer area. You can also press Alt + H. |
|  | Fits the current image to the width of the Document Viewer area. You can also press Alt + W. |

## Updating The Document Class

The Document List enables you to select a document class for a specific document. You can view the list of available document classes for the current batch and select the relevant document class.

You can also select the document class with the Snippet Viewer on the Document Viewer. For more information, see Assigning a Document Class with the Snippet Viewer.

For more information on document status and assignment of a document class based on the document status, see Document Status Types.

To update the document class:

1.  Click a batch record on the batch list page or press Tab to select a batch record, and then press Enter. The Document List view is displayed on the left of the Composite Verification View.
2.  Select the relevant document area for which you want to select a document class, or press the Up and Down arrow keys to navigate to the relevant document.
3.  Click the arrow to expand the document class drop-down list or press F7. The available document classes are displayed.
4.  Type or select the relevant document class from the document class drop-down list or use the keyboard shortcuts specified next to each document class. The document class is saved.  Note:
To cancel any erroneous text, click the Revert icon beside the document class or press Esc.

## Uploading A Sample Bar Code Document

Uploading a sample document containing a bar code helps you to configure the bar code profile settings and ensures the bar codes are detected correctly. The supported file formats are JPG and PNG.

To upload a sample document containing a bar code:

1.  On the Create Bar Code Profile screen, click browse to upload a sample document. The File dialog box is displayed.
Alternatively, you can drag and drop the relevant bar code image or file in the Drag & drop files to Import box.

The bar code type, value, and scanning area details are automatically generated based on the bar code area you specify.

2.  In the File dialog box, select the bar code file you want to upload and click Open. The Select bar code area dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  From the list of bar codes available in the uploaded document, select the bar code type to use for separation.
When selecting the bar code type, consider the following:

-
All the detected bar codes are highlighted in yellow. The selected bar code is highlighted in blue.

-
If you are unsure of the exact location of the bar code on the page, you can select Scan whole image to configure the bar code separation profile to scan the entire page to detect the bar code (see step 4 for details).

-
If you want to manually set the bar code area, type, and value instead of using the automatically detected settings from the sample document, you can select Manual as the type (see step 5 for details).

-
Depending on your selections, a nudgeable box is displayed around the automatically detected bar code or the area you draw manually. You can resize the box by clicking and dragging its corners. For accurate bar code detection during scanning, it is recommended that you make the size of the nudgeable box larger than the bar code.

4.  To configure a profile to scan the entire page for bar codes, select Scan whole image. Selecting this option sets the Starting Position From Left and Starting Position From Top fields to 0 and the Width and Height fields to 100.
5.  To manually select the bar code area on the page, select Manual. This allows you to draw a nudgeable box around the region of the page where the application needs to search for a bar code.
When using the Manual option, also note the following:

-
The Manual option is selected by default if no bar codes are detected.

-
After uploading the sample file, you must select the appropriate bar code type from the Bar Code Type drop-down list on the Create Separation Profile screen.

- The nudgeable box appears on the first click. Click again to resize the nudgeable box.
6.  Click Save.  Note:
The sample image you have uploaded is not stored or reused for future configurations; only the settings that have been created with the image are stored. You must upload the image again if you want to use it to edit the same profile.

## Verifying Document Metadata

You can verify or review document metadata with the Snippet Viewer on the Document Viewer.

For more information, see Verifying Document Metadata with the Snippet Viewer.

For verification of extracted results in documents that contain a date field, see Date Handling and Validation.

For verification of extracted results in documents containing currency fields, see Currency Handling and Validation.

To verify document metadata:

1.  Navigate to the relevant field which you want to verify and do one of the following:
-
On the Document Viewer, double-click the relevant text which you want to map to the corresponding metadata field.

-
Draw a rectangle around the text that you want to map to the metadata field.

2.  Type the text that you want to map and then select it.  Note:
When you begin typing, the auto-completion feature prompts and suggests the best match among all of the words or candidates available. The auto-selected text also appears highlighted on the Document Viewer. However, multiple words are not supported during auto completion in extraction verification. To override auto-completion, continue typing the desired text or rubber band the whole word.

3.  Defocus the field by clicking outside it or pressing Enter or Tab. The field data is saved.

## Verifying Document Metadata With The Snippet Viewer

The Snippet Viewer is a rectangular floating component which is displayed above the Document Viewer. The Snippet Viewer enables you to view and navigate between each document metadata field and quickly verify each field. You can also draw a rectangle around the required text snipper and view the corresponding content on the Snippet Viewer. You can work with the Snipper Viewer even when the Document List and Metadata Panel are collapsed.

To verify document metadata with the Snippet Viewer:

1.  To change how the Snippet Viewer is displayed, do any of the following:
-
To drag the Snippet Viewer to a new position, click and hold the Drag snippet viewer icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
To collapse the Snippet Viewer, click the Collapse snippet viewer icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
To expand the Snippet Viewer, click the Expand snipper viewer icon in the lower-right corner of the Document Viewer.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  On the Snippet Viewer, navigate to the relevant metadata field which you want to verify and do one of the following:
-
In the document page, double-click the relevant text which you want to map to the corresponding metadata field.

-
Type the text which you want to map to the metadata field and then select it.

-
Draw a rectangle around the text which you want to map to the metadata field.

Note:
You can also navigate to the relevant metadata field on the Snippet Viewer by clicking the Ctrl and arrow keys. When you start to type, the auto-completion feature completes the word and suggests the best match among all of the words or candidates available. The auto-selected text also appears highlighted on the Document Viewer. However, multiple words are not supported during auto completion in extraction verification. To override auto-completion, continue typing the desired text or rubber band the whole word.

3.  Defocus the field by clicking outside it or pressing Enter or Tab. The field data is saved.

## Viewing The Batch List

When you select the relevant Content Queue, the list of batches mapped to the Content Queue is displayed. You can select the Show batches to be verified check box to filter the batch list so that only those batches that can be verified are displayed. You can open a batch only if it is in the Ready for Verification or Verification state.

The following columns are displayed on the batch list page. Each column provides some batch-specific information.

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Batch Name | The name of the batch. |
| Document/Pages | The total number of documents and pages within the batch. |
| Status | The current status of the batch. |
| Created by | The name of the user who created the batch. |
| Owner | The name of the user who is currently working on the batch. For more information, refer to Batch Concurrency. |
| Creation Date & Time | The date and time on which the batch was created. |

## Working With The Document List

The Document List enables you to perform the following activities:

- View the name of the Content Queue to which the batch belongs.
-
View the total number of documents in the selected batch

-
View the total number of pages in a document

-
View a list of document thumbnails inside the selected batch with the document class on top

-
Navigate from one document to another using the Document List toolbar

For more information, refer to The Document List Toolbar

-
Resize the thumbnail images for quickly working with document pages

For more information, refer to Resizing Thumbnail Images

-
Assign or modify a document class

For more information, refer to Updating the Document Class

-
Drag and drop a document page from its current position to a different position within the same document

For more information, refer to Reordering Pages.

-
Append a document to the previous document

For more information, refer to Appending a Document to the Previous Document

-
Append selected pages of a document with the previous document

For more information, refer to Appending Selected Pages to the Previous Document

-
Split and create a new document from selected pages of an existing document

For more information, refer to Splitting and Creating a Document with Selected Pages

-
Split a document from the selected page to its last page and create a new document

For more information, refer to Splitting a Document from the Current Page

-
Delete selected pages from a document

For more information, refer to Deleting Pages from a Document

-
Delete an entire document

For more information, refer to Deleting a Document

-
Submit a batch after classification and verification is completed

For more information, refer to Submitting a Batch

-
Collapse the Document List

-
Directly load the next available batch after submitting the current batch

For more information, refer to Opening the Next Invalid Batch.

## Working With The Document Viewer

The Document Viewer enables you to perform the following activities:

-
View an enlarged version of each page of every document inside your batch.

-
Navigate between the pages and customize the page view settings as per your requirements. For more information, see The Document Viewer Toolbar.

-
Verify document metadata using the Snippet Viewer. The Snippet Viewer is a rectangular floating component that enables you to view and navigate through all the document metadata fields, and quickly view and verify the fields

Note:
You can work with the Snippet Viewer even when the Document List and Metadata Panel are collapsed.

For more information, see Verifying Document Metadata with the Snippet Viewer.
